title: Second marriage during pendency of divorce - the legal consequences
p1:
A divorce case is pending. Can either spouse simply move on and marry someone else?

Many people believe that filing for divorce means the marriage has practically ended. Legally, that is not always true.
p2:
A pending divorce does not dissolve a marriage. Until the court grants a final divorce decree, the spouses are still legally married.

The surprising part: a second marriage during this period can create serious legal consequences, including being treated as an offence in many situations.
p3:
The practical lesson is simple: separation and divorce are not the same thing. A person may have been living separately for years, but a new marriage before the legal end of the first marriage can create complications for everyone involved.

Before taking such a step, checking the legal status of the first marriage is essential.
conclusion: Did you know that living separately for years does not automatically give someone the legal right to remarry? What are your views on this distinction between separation and divorce?
